skill.name: event-sourcing-architect
skill.description: Expert in event sourcing, CQRS, and event-driven architecture patterns. Masters event store design, projection building, saga orchestration, and eventual consistency patterns. Use PROACTIVELY for event-sourced systems, audit trails, or temporal queries.

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported eight weak cryptographic algorithm matches in SKILL.md, including a duplicate on line 3. Manual review found architecture guidance only, with no cryptographic algorithm usage, code execution, network access, filesystem access, or prompt injection. The detections are false positives, and the skill is safe to publish.

content.value_statement: Event-sourced systems are difficult to model because history, projections, and consistency must stay aligned. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code plan event stores, CQRS boundaries, projections, and sagas.
actual_capabilities: Identifies aggregate boundaries and event streams for event-sourced domains.
Designs immutable domain events and command handling flows.
Plans CQRS read models, projections, and rebuild strategies.
Models sagas and process managers for cross-aggregate workflows.
Recommends event versioning, snapshotting, and idempotent handlers.
limitations: Does not provide executable framework code by itself.
Does not replace domain expert validation of business events.
Does not guarantee immediate consistency for all workflows.
Requires an event store and projection infrastructure to implement designs.

use_cases.title: Model a new financial ledger
use_cases.description: A backend engineer can define aggregates, events, commands, and projections for a ledger that needs complete history.

use_cases.title: Modernize a CRUD workflow
use_cases.description: A software architect can evaluate whether event sourcing improves auditability, rollback, and reporting for a complex workflow.

use_cases.title: Plan microservice coordination
use_cases.description: A platform team can design sagas, correlation IDs, and idempotent handlers for distributed business processes.

prompt_templates.title: Assess fit
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this domain and tell me whether event sourcing is appropriate. Explain the tradeoffs, risks, and simpler alternatives.

prompt_templates.title: Design aggregates
prompt_templates.prompt: Given these business rules, propose aggregate boundaries, commands, events, invariants, and event streams for an event-sourced design.

prompt_templates.title: Plan projections
prompt_templates.prompt: Design CQRS projections for these query requirements. Include rebuild strategy, idempotency rules, and eventual consistency handling.

prompt_templates.title: Orchestrate workflows
prompt_templates.prompt: Design sagas for this cross-service workflow. Include compensating actions, correlation IDs, failure handling, and event versioning.

output_examples.input: Design event sourcing for customer subscription billing.
output_examples.output: The skill produces aggregate boundaries, billing commands, immutable subscription events, invoice projections, and saga steps for payment failures.

output_examples.input: Review a proposed event stream for order fulfillment.
output_examples.output: The skill identifies missing facts, unclear event names, projection rebuild risks, and handlers that need idempotency.

output_examples.input: Plan temporal queries for account status history.
output_examples.output: The skill outlines event streams, snapshot points, projection choices, and query patterns for reconstructing state at a chosen time.

best_practices: Use past-tense events that describe completed business facts.
Keep event handlers idempotent and safe to replay.
Version events early and document migration rules.
anti_patterns: Using event sourcing when simple CRUD fully meets the domain needs.
Changing or deleting committed production events.
Building projections without a tested rebuild path.

faq.question: What does this skill help design?
faq.answer: It helps design event-sourced systems, CQRS read models, projections, sagas, snapshots, and event versioning plans.

faq.question: Does it generate production code?
faq.answer: It focuses on architecture and implementation planning. You can ask for code, but the skill itself contains guidance only.

faq.question: When should I avoid event sourcing?
faq.answer: Avoid it when the domain is simple, CRUD is sufficient, or strong immediate consistency is required everywhere.

faq.question: Can it help with audit trails?
faq.answer: Yes. It is useful when systems need durable histories, temporal queries, or traceable business changes.

faq.question: Does it handle distributed workflows?
faq.answer: Yes. It can plan sagas, process managers, compensating actions, and correlation IDs for cross-aggregate workflows.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed skill is instruction-only and shows no code execution, network access, filesystem access, or prompt injection.
